message:
feat(fcp-core): add RetryDirective enum (Immediate/Backoff/RetryAfter/Terminal) + Display pinning test

Implements bead flywheel_connectors-ynl5u ("RetryDirective Display
formatting"). Introduces a public retry-classification enum so transport
adapters can emit a single canonical directive instead of returning
ad-hoc booleans + optional delays, and pins the resulting Display
contract.

Production change (crates/fcp-core/src/capability.rs):

- New `pub enum RetryDirective` with four variants:
  * Immediate          — retry without waiting
  * Backoff            — retry using the caller's configured policy
  * RetryAfter(Duration) — retry after a concrete delay (matches HTTP
                          Retry-After delta-seconds form)
  * Terminal           — do not retry
- Derives Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash so the directive
  slots into both fast hot-path branching and HashMap-keyed retry
  registries.
- `RetryDirective::parse_retry_after(value: &str)` parses the
  delta-seconds form of the HTTP Retry-After header, returning
  RetryAfter(Duration) on success or a new structured
  RetryDirectiveParseError on empty / non-numeric / overflow input.
  Date-form parsing is intentionally NOT included here; the doc
  comment notes it belongs at transport adapters that can supply
  the clock.
- `RetryDirective::retry_after(self) -> Option<Duration>` is a
  const accessor that returns the carried delay only for the
  RetryAfter variant; Immediate / Backoff / Terminal return None.
- `impl fmt::Display for RetryDirective` writes "immediate",
  "backoff", "retry_after:<seconds>s", or "terminal" — the snake-
  cased lowercase form pinned by the new test.

Pinning test (crates/fcp-core/tests/retry_directive_display.rs, new):

- Pins each variant's Display rendering exactly, including the
  RetryAfter(Duration) format that includes the seconds value.
- Round-trips parse_retry_after across a representative set of
  inputs and asserts each round-trips back through Display.
- Pins the retry_after() accessor: Some(delay) only for
  RetryAfter, None for the other three variants.
- Asserts the parse error path on empty / non-numeric / overflow
  input.

Beads sync: marks ynl5u as closed in the same commit since the test
landed alongside the implementation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-l25","title":"fcp.terraform: Resource import for existing infrastructure","description":"# fcp.terraform: Resource import for existing infrastructure (FCP2)

## Goal
Allow controlled `terraform import` so agents can bring existing infrastructure under state management.

Import mutates state and can be risky; require approval gating.

## Scope
- Import a resource into state with explicit address + provider id.
- Validate inputs and prevent injection into CLI arguments.
- Emit receipts/audit for the import action.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `terraform.import` (dangerous)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- Sandbox profile restricts filesystem + process execution.
- NetworkConstraints default deny unless provider APIs are explicitly allowed.
- Approval gating recommended.

## Deliverables
- Operation schema:
  - `terraform.import`
- Input validation:
  - strict address grammar
  - strict provider id format

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Unit tests with a terraform shim for:
  - arg construction
  - approval gating
  - audit/receipt emission

## Acceptance criteria
- Imports are mechanically authorized, safe, and auditable.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-l4gxo","title":"[PERF] fcp-webhook::cleanup_seen_events: every delivery takes the replay-cache write lock","description":"crates/fcp-webhook/src/handler.rs:193-219 and 268-319. check_replay() and claim_event() call cleanup_seen_events() on every delivery, and cleanup_seen_events() immediately acquires seen_events.write() before it even checks whether a cleanup pass is due. That makes the steady-state hot path serialize concurrent webhook deliveries on a write lock even when the common-case exit is an immediate no-op. Once per minute it also runs retain() across the full replay cache while holding that same lock. Proposed improvement: gate cleanup scheduling with an atomic or read-side timestamp first, and only take the write lock when a cleanup pass is actually due.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:57:56.831428Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:25:20.804138Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:25:20.803178Z","close_reason":"Replay cleanup now gates write-lock pruning behind an atomic due-check instead of taking the replay-cache write lock on every delivery","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-l4vs9","title":"[REVIEW-FINAL][MEDIUM] fcp-telemetry drops valid tracestate members with optional OWS","description":"crates/fcp-telemetry/src/tracing_layer.rs:208-223 validates tracestate via value.split(',') and immediately split_once('=') on each member without trimming RFC/W3C optional whitespace around commas. Valid inbound headers like 'vendor=a, tenant@sys=b' are therefore rejected and tracestate reflection is silently dropped for compliant proxies/SDKs that emit OWS. Attack/regression scenario: cross-service traces lose vendor state only when an intermediary normalizes headers with spaces after commas, making propagation flaky and hard to debug. Proposed mitigation: trim leading/trailing SP/HTAB around each list-member before key/value validation while preserving the existing total-length and member-count caps.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:55:55.603627Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T22:09:39.617459Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T22:09:39.617144Z","close_reason":"Accepted valid tracestate members with optional SP/HTAB around commas while preserving malformed member rejection","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-l85v","title":"TEST: fcp-security Unit Test Suite","description":"# TEST: fcp-security Unit Test Suite

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for security testing and audit framework.

## Test Categories

### Capability Token Validation
- [ ] Ed25519 signature verification
- [ ] Token expiration checks
- [ ] Issuer/audience validation
- [ ] Capability scope enforcement
- [ ] Resource constraint validation

### Zone Security
- [ ] Zone boundary enforcement
- [ ] Trust level validation
- [ ] Cross-zone access denial
- [ ] Zone hierarchy traversal

### Provenance Tracking
- [ ] Taint label propagation
- [ ] Origin tracking
- [ ] Causal chain verification

### Input Validation
- [ ] SQL injection patterns
- [ ] XSS patterns
- [ ] Command injection patterns
- [ ] Path traversal patterns
- [ ] SSRF patterns

### Cryptographic Operations
- [ ] Key generation
- [ ] Signature creation/verification
- [ ] Secure random generation
- [ ] Constant-time comparisons

### Audit Logging
- [ ] Security event capture
- [ ] Tamper-evident logging
- [ ] Correlation ID tracking

## Coverage Target: >98% (security-critical)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T17:12:48.845571789Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-15T08:22:24.386898237Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T08:22:24.386898237Z","close_reason":"Superseded - parent bead fcp-security is closed. Security testing is now covered by crypto tests (2ukh), capability tests (nlz4), and zone crypto tests (9smo).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-l85v","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1j7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-l9wf","title":"TEST: FCPS/FCPC Protocol Unit Tests (Frame Parsing, AEAD, MAC Verification)","description":"# TEST: FCPS/FCPC Protocol Unit Tests


## Goal
Prove FCPS/FCPC framing, parsing, and crypto verification are correct and bounded under adversarial inputs.

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for wire protocol frame parsing and cryptographic verification.

## FCPS (Symbol Plane) Tests

### Frame Parsing
- [ ] Valid frame parses correctly
- [ ] Magic bytes validation ("FCPS")
- [ ] Version field bounds checking
- [ ] Flag combinations parsed correctly
- [ ] Symbol count matches actual symbols
- [ ] Total payload length matches content
- [ ] Zone ID hash is 32 bytes exactly
- [ ] Object ID is 32 bytes exactly

### Symbol Record Parsing
- [ ] ESI (encoding symbol identifier) extraction
- [ ] K parameter extraction
- [ ] Data length matches symbol_size
- [ ] Auth tag is 16 bytes exactly

### Datagram Envelope
- [ ] Session ID extraction (16 bytes)
- [ ] Sequence number parsing (u64 LE)
- [ ] MAC extraction (16 bytes)
- [ ] MAC verification success with correct key
- [ ] MAC verification failure with wrong key
- [ ] MAC verification failure with tampered frame
- [ ] Direction byte in MAC context

### Per-Symbol AEAD
- [ ] Sender key derivation matches spec
- [ ] Nonce derivation: frame_seq || esi
- [ ] AAD binding includes all required fields
- [ ] Decrypt success with correct key
- [ ] Decrypt failure with wrong key
- [ ] Decrypt failure with tampered ciphertext
- [ ] Decrypt failure with wrong AAD

### Frame Flags
- [ ] REQUIRES_ACK flag handling
- [ ] COMPRESSED flag handling
- [ ] ENCRYPTED flag handling
- [ ] STREAMING + STREAM_END combination
- [ ] HAS_CAP_TOKEN flag handling
- [ ] ZONE_CROSSING flag handling
- [ ] CONTROL_PLANE flag handling
- [ ] Invalid flag combinations rejected

## FCPC (Control Plane) Tests

### Frame Structure
- [ ] Canonical CBOR envelope parsing
- [ ] Object type discrimination
- [ ] Length prefix validation
- [ ] Backpressure frame parsing

### Control Objects
- [ ] ControlPlaneObject type dispatch
- [ ] Session control messages
- [ ] Capability objects
- [ ] Revocation objects
- [ ] Checkpoint objects

## DoS Resistance Tests
- [ ] Oversized frame rejected
- [ ] Invalid symbol count rejected
- [ ] Frame exceeding MTU rejected
- [ ] Memory bounds on malformed input

## Golden Vectors
Each test category needs:
- Valid frame bytes (hex)
- Expected parsed structure
- Invalid frame bytes that MUST be rejected

## Fuzz Targets
- FCPS frame parsing
- FCPC frame parsing
- Symbol record parsing
- Datagram envelope parsing

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "test_name": "...",
  "protocol": "fcps|fcpc",
  "operation": "parse|verify|encode",
  "frame_size": 1234,
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "error_code": "optional"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All parsing paths have test coverage
- DoS protections verified
- Golden vectors match exactly
- Fuzz targets find no crashes

","notes":"Tests validated: all 72 fcp-protocol tests pass. Coverage confirmed for: FCPS frame parsing/flags/symbols, FCPC framing/AEAD/replay, MAC verification, golden vectors. Fuzz targets exist for all components. Blocked waiting for 1n78.13 completion.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindySnow","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T07:55:24.993533714Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T08:30:23.929675939Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T08:30:23.929675939Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added comprehensive FCPS/FCPC protocol unit tests including DoS resistance (symbol count overflow, MTU violation, memory bounds), replay protection, and reference vector generation. All 50 golden vector tests pass (28 FCPC + 22 FCPS). Implementation dependency (1n78.13) is still in progress but tests are self-contained and complete.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp2","mvp","protocol","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-l9wf","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-l9wf","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-l9wf","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lcejk","title":"fix-SLACK: replace always-allowed simulate stub","description":"Slack handle_simulate currently deserializes SimulateRequest and always returns SimulateResponse::allowed. Make it mirror invoke preconditions by denying unknown operations, invalid inputs/resource bindings, unconfigured/unhandshaken state, and capability token verification failures.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T21:39:07.448131Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T21:44:36.841460Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T21:44:36.840929Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fix","security"],"comments":[{"id":3055,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lcejk","author":"jemanuel","text":"8afba9ca implements Slack simulate preflight checks: supported operation gating, required input validation, configure/handshake denial, resource-bound capability verification, and six regression tests. Verification: cd connectors/slack && CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cod4-fixed cargo test --lib simulate passed 6/6; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cod4-fixed cargo test --tests passed 219 lib + 3 bin + 52 integration + 4 live_verification. ubs --staged --only=rust ran on staged Slack file; fmt/clippy/check/test-build clean, exit 1 from existing broad heuristics/test panic inventory.","created_at":"2026-04-27T21:44:36Z"}]}
